text: OIL SETTLEMENT CRASH: Brent settles $90.38 (-9% on day, +13.3% from pre-war). WTI $83.85 (-11.5% on day). Biggest single-day drop since April 8 ceasefire. Markets celebrating Hormuz opening + deal optimism. S&P 500 record 7,126. Nasdaq 13-day winning streak — longest since 1992. Dow +869 pts. BUT: Maersk says needs 'further guarantees' before resuming Hormuz transit. Insurance still 5x normal. Zero oil tankers transiting.
verificationNote: Reuters, CNBC, TheStreet, Investopedia confirmed settlement prices and market records.

text: Day 49 PM roundup: TRUMP PROHIBITS Israel bombing Lebanon. Israeli drone kills 1 post-ceasefire (first fatality). Brent settles $90.38 (-9%), WTI $83.85 (-11.5%) — biggest crash since April 8. S&P record 7,126. Nasdaq 13-day streak. European Hormuz mission (~30 countries). $20B uranium deal under discussion. Katz: Israel holds 10km security zone indefinitely. Guterres: Hormuz opening 'step in right direction.' Gas $4.093. Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 5 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Reuters, Axios, CBC News, CNBC, Politico EU, UN News, AAA.

text: RAS ISSA STRIKE: US forces struck Ras Issa oil port in Hodeidah, Yemen — 80 killed, 171+ injured per latest toll. Deadliest single US strike in Operation Rough Rider. First attack on Houthi-controlled oil facility. Massive fireballs destroyed tanker trucks and port infrastructure. Guterres 'gravely concerned.' HRW calling apparent war crime. Euromed Monitor demands investigation.
verificationNote: PBS News, EuroMed Monitor, Al Mayadeen confirmed strike and casualty figures.

text: HOUSE WAR POWERS: Resolution fails 213-214 — ONE vote short. Only Rep. Massie (R-KY) crossed party lines. Senate blocked 47-52 on April 15. 60-day War Powers clock approaching April 29. Fifth time Congress has attempted to restrain Trump's Iran war authority. Bipartisan antiwar coalition growing but still insufficient.
verificationNote: NBC News, Democracy Now confirmed vote tally and party breakdown.

text: OIL REBOUND: Brent rebounds to $95.86 (+20.1% from pre-war), WTI $90.79 (+20.2%) after massive April 17 crash. Markets realizing US CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports continues despite Hormuz opening. Iran state media walks back Araghchi — says he 'misspoke.' Gold surges to $4,867/oz (+41.7% YoY). Sanctions waiver expires tomorrow April 19. EIA raised 2026 Brent forecast to $96/bbl average.
verificationNote: Trading Economics, Investing.com, EIA confirmed oil rebound. Sherwood News confirmed Araghchi walkback.

text: PEZESHKIAN LETTER: Iran president publishes second open letter to American people — 'Iran harbours no enmity toward ordinary Americans.' Calls portrayal of Iran as threat 'inconsistent with history.' Separately: Iran Parliament Speaker Qalibaf warns 'If blockade continues, Hormuz will not remain open.' IRGC Navy warns any military vessel approaching strait = ceasefire violation.
verificationNote: Iran LiveUAMap, Tribune India confirmed letter. NBC News confirmed Qalibaf warning.

text: Day 50 PM roundup: RAS ISSA — US strikes Yemen oil port, 80 killed (deadliest single US strike). HOUSE — War powers fails 213-214 (one vote). OIL — Brent rebounds $95.86, WTI $90.79. Iran walks back Hormuz opening. PEZESHKIAN — 2nd letter to Americans. GOLD — $4,867/oz. Gas $4.076 (ticking down). Sanctions waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 4 days. Next talks Monday Islamabad.
verificationNote: Compiled from PBS, NBC News, Trading Economics, Iran LiveUAMap, AAA.

text: HORMUZ CHAOS: Iran reverses Strait of Hormuz opening within hours. Ghalibaf scrapped reopening deal citing US 'untrustworthiness.' IRGC gunboats fired on tanker 20 miles NE of Oman — UKMTO confirmed, no injuries. Two more merchant vessels hit. Armed Forces accuses US of 'piracy and maritime robbery.' Oil surging on renewed uncertainty.
verificationNote: NBC News, CBS News, Irish Times, The National confirmed reversal and gunboat incident.

text: TRUMP SIGNALS: At Arizona Turning Point rally Trump announced Hormuz 'fully open' (already reversed by Iran). On Air Force One said 'some pretty good news 20 minutes ago' but declined to elaborate. Warned ceasefire may end without deal by Wednesday. Blockade 'remains in full force.' Next talks expected Monday Islamabad.

DAY 50 AM ROUNDUP: Iran pushes back on Trump deal claims. Hormuz shut down again — Iran cites US 'piracy.' Enriched uranium sticking point — US says 'going very well,' Iran denies agreeing to handover. CENTCOM: 21 vessels turned back. Lebanon ceasefire violations continue. US sanctions 7 Iraqi militia commanders. Brent $97.68 (+22.3%).
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, NBC News, Tribune India, US Treasury confirmed.

text: NUCLEAR STALEMATE: Iran FM rejects Trump's enriched uranium handover claim — 'will not be transferred anywhere.' US demanded 20-year moratorium, Iran countered with 5 years. ~450kg at 60% purity at issue. $20B cash-for-uranium framework still under discussion. Next talks expected Monday Islamabad. Ceasefire expires April 22.

Day 50 AM roundup: HORMUZ — Iran reverses opening, IRGC gunboats fire on tanker (no injuries). Ghalibaf: 'America's untrustworthiness.' TRUMP — 'good news' at Arizona rally, blockade 'in full force.' NUCLEAR — Iran rejects uranium handover. SANCTIONS — 7 Iraqi militia commanders designated. OIL — Brent $97.68 (+22.3%), WTI $93.74 (+24.1%). Gas $4.076. Waiver expires April 19. Ceasefire April 22 — 4 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from NBC News, CBS News, Irish Times, The National, Al-Monitor, Jerusalem Post, Tribune India, Bloomberg, US Treasury.

text: HORMUZ FRESH INCIDENTS: TankerTrackers.com confirms 3 separate merchant vessel incidents April 18 — Indian-flagged supertanker carrying Iraqi oil fired on by IRGC gunboats (turned back), container vessel struck by unknown projectile (container damage, no casualties), third tanker hit by gunfire. UKMTO logged first incident 09:20 UTC, ~20nm NE of Oman. Iran's joint military command: strait 'returned to previous state under strict management.'
verificationNote: Axios, Military.com, Ship & Bunker, Al Jazeera confirmed all three incidents and UKMTO source.

text: CENTCOM ESCALATED POSTURE: CENTCOM released imagery of AH-64 Apache attack helicopters conducting patrols over Strait of Hormuz from USS Tripoli ARG. Signal follows Iran's reimposed strait closure. Apaches provide anti-surface capability vs. IRGC fast-attack boats — designed specifically for the small-boat threat. USS Bush CSG en route via Cape Horn, will make it the third US carrier group in theater.
verificationNote: The Aviationist, Stars and Stripes confirmed Apache imagery and CSG deployment.

text: TREASURY 'FINANCIAL BOMBING CAMPAIGN': Treasury Sec. Bessent at White House Tax Day briefing vows escalation of secondary sanctions as 'financial equivalent of a bombing campaign' on Iran. Targets: Chinese refiners buying Iranian oil, shadow fleet operators, front companies. Iraq Iranian-gas sanctions waiver expires April 19 and will not be renewed — cuts off Iraq's electrical grid funding stream to Iran.
verificationNote: PBS, State Dept confirmed. Bessent quote from WH briefing.

text: DIPLOMACY WATCH: Iranian official tells Reuters Tehran is studying fresh US proposals delivered via Pakistan track. Insists US naval blockade of Iranian ports is 'violation' of April 8 ceasefire. Next round of talks confirmed for Monday April 20 in Islamabad. Ceasefire expires April 22 — negotiating window collapses to 2 days. Trump on interview: 'Maybe I won't extend [ceasefire], but blockade is going to remain.'
verificationNote: Irish Times, OPB, Fox News confirmed.

text: Day 50 PM roundup: HORMUZ — 3 merchant vessel incidents confirmed (Indian tanker w/Iraqi oil, container ship hit, 3rd tanker gunfire). CENTCOM Apache patrols. OIL — Brent rebounds $95.86 (+20.1%), WTI $90.79 (+20.2%). BESSENT — 'financial bombing campaign' secondary sanctions. IRAQ WAIVER expires April 19. DIPLOMACY — Iran studies US proposals, Islamabad talks Monday. LEBANON TRUCE Day 2 strained — Lebanese Army logs violations. Gas $4.076. Ceasefire expires April 22 — 4 days.
